name: Pooch (Scientific Data File Downloader)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD-3); Fatiando a Terra project

auth.notes: Download with optional authentication for private repos

performance.notes: Network download speed; caching prevents re-downloads

agent_readiness.notes: Scientific data file manager with caching and integrity checking. import pooch. fname = pooch.retrieve(url='https://...', known_hash='sha256:abc123'). Auto-caches in platform-appropriate dir. Re-downloads only if hash mismatch. Extractors: pooch.Untar(), pooch.Unzip(). GitHub release downloads. Versioned data registry. pooch.create() for managing datasets in libraries. Used by scikit-learn, matplotlib sample data, ObsPy, Fatiando. The standard way scientific Python libraries ship sample data.

gotchas: Hash verification only catches corruption, not malicious files
Cache location is platform-dependent (user home dir)
best_when: Scientific Python libraries shipping sample/test datasets
Downloading large data files with SHA256 integrity verification
Caching remote datasets for reproducible scientific analysis
Managing versioned data alongside code in libraries
avoid_when: Production data pipelines (use proper ETL tools)
Very large datasets (hundreds of GB) — use DVC or cloud storage
summary: Pooch manages scientific data file downloads with SHA256 integrity checking and local caching. pooch.retrieve(url=..., known_hash='sha256:...') downloads and verifies once, then serves from cache. Auto-extracts zip/tar. GitHub release support. Used by scikit-learn, matplotlib, ObsPy for sample data distribution. The standard way scientific Python libraries ship datasets with reproducible checksums.
